projectName: The Shattered Realms — Dev Test Suite

timeline.title: Book 1: The Ember Crown
timeline.desc: The opening book of the series. Follows Kael Dawnbreaker as he discovers the ancient Ember Crown buried beneath the ruins of the Ashen Keep. Three kingdoms collapse in its wake.

timeline.title: Chapter 1: The Waking Dark
timeline.desc: Kael is summoned from his exile in the Thornwood. He arrives at the capital just as the first quake splits the palace courtyard.

timeline.title: Kael receives the summons scroll
timeline.desc: A silver-threaded scroll arrives at his cottage at dawn. The seal is the queen's own — unbroken.

timeline.title: Kael departs Thornwood
First quake splits the palace courtyard
timeline.desc: Cracks form in a perfect circle — 40 feet wide. Soldiers refuse to approach the edge.

timeline.title: Kael meets Queen Serath for the first time in 11 years
Chapter 2: Beneath the Ashen Keep
timeline.desc: The excavation of the keep's lower vaults begins under the queen's orders. Kael leads the descent with reluctance.

timeline.title: Excavation team assembled — 12 people
Vault door discovered — no visible hinges or lock
timeline.desc: The door hums when touched. Mira the archivist identifies the script as pre-Sundering glyphs.

timeline.title: Three members of the team disappear overnight
Kael opens the vault using his blood — unplanned, instinctive
timeline.desc: He doesn't know why he cuts his palm. He doesn't know why the door recognises him. Neither does anyone else.

timeline.title: The Ember Crown is found on a throne of black glass
Chapter 3: The Crown Does Not Sleep
Kael hears the crown speak — only he can hear it
timeline.desc: It doesn't use words. It uses memories — specifically ones Kael has never had.

timeline.title: Queen Serath attempts to wear the crown — repelled violently
The capital's river runs red for six hours
Mira deciphers a fragment: 'Only the forgotten heir may carry fire'
timeline.desc: This is the first direct textual evidence that Kael is not who he thinks he is. Mira keeps it to herself for now.

timeline.title: Chapter 4: Treason and Ash
timeline.desc: The Lord Commander accuses Kael of orchestrating the quakes. Political fallout fractures the queen's council.

timeline.title: Lord Commander Vayne files formal charges against Kael
Kael escapes the palace with the crown and Mira's help
timeline.desc: They ride east through the Ashfields. Mira tells him about the fragment. Kael doesn't speak for two hours.

timeline.title: Vayne burns the lower vaults — destroying most of the pre-Sundering archive
Book 2: Salt and Iron
timeline.desc: A shorter transitional book told from three alternating POVs: Kael on the run, Queen Serath managing collapse, and an unnamed figure watching from the eastern border.

timeline.title: Kael crosses the Ashfields alone — Mira captured at the border
timeline.desc: He doesn't go back. This is a deliberate and painful choice. He tells himself he'll return for her.

timeline.title: Queen Serath dissolves the High Council
Serath opens negotiations with the Silt Republic — previously sworn enemies
timeline.desc: Desperate times. The Republic wants trade access to the northern grain roads. Serath is out of leverage but doesn't show it.

timeline.title: The unnamed watcher is revealed to be observing Kael specifically — not the crown
Second quake — this time centred on the eastern border, not the capital
timeline.desc: Measuring stones place the epicentre exactly where Kael crossed the night before. Correlation noted by three independent sources.

timeline.title: Kael hears the crown say a name: Sorvenne
Book 2 ends — Kael arrives at the gates of Delthara, the city that should not exist
timeline.desc: Delthara was destroyed 200 years ago. Every map since shows only a crater. Kael is standing at its gate.

timeline.title: Book 3: The City That Remembers
Chapter 1: Delthara
timeline.desc: Kael enters the impossible city. It is populated — but the people do not age, and none of them remember the destruction.

timeline.title: Kael enters Delthara unchallenged
A child recognises the crown and runs
timeline.desc: She doesn't scream. She just turns and disappears into the crowd. Kael follows but loses her in seconds.

timeline.title: Kael learns no one in Delthara has a shadow
He is brought before the Remembrant — the city's single governing voice
timeline.desc: The Remembrant speaks in plural. 'We have been expecting the crown. We did not expect you to be carrying it.'

timeline.title: Chapter 2: What the Fire Remembers
The Remembrant reveals the crown's origin — forged from the grief of a dead god
timeline.desc: Specifically, a god of endings. The crown does not grant power — it accumulates consequence. Every choice made near it becomes permanent in ways the chooser cannot undo.

timeline.title: Kael is shown a vision of Sorvenne — she is a woman who burned Delthara the first time
The crown reacts — first time it has physically moved on its own
Kael realises Sorvenne is still alive — and she is the unnamed watcher from Book 2
timeline.desc: This is the emotional gut-punch of Book 3. Everything recontextualises. She wasn't watching the crown. She was watching him because she's been waiting for the heir to appear.

timeline.title: Chapter 3: The Third Burning
timeline.desc: Sorvenne arrives at Delthara's gates. The city knows her. The city is afraid.

timeline.title: Sorvenne enters without resistance — the gates open for her
First conversation between Kael and Sorvenne
timeline.desc: She is not a villain. She is exhausted. She has been alive for 200 years, waiting for someone who can end what she started. 'I burned this city to stop a worse fire. I was wrong. Now I need you to finish it.'

timeline.title: The Remembrant shatters — Sorvenne's presence breaks the collective binding
Delthara begins to fade — the city only existed because it was remembered
Kael puts on the crown for the first time
timeline.desc: He does not do it willingly. The choice is taken from him by the one permanent consequence the crown has already set in motion: the moment he opened the vault with his blood, this was always next.

timeline.title: Book 4: Everything That Burns
timeline.desc: The final book. Kael now carries both the crown and the weight of Sorvenne's confession. Three armies are converging on the Ashfields. Mira has been freed but changed. The world is ending in slow motion.

timeline.title: Chapter 1: Three Armies
Vayne's army marches from the capital — now acting as self-appointed regent
timeline.desc: He has declared Serath unfit to rule after she opened negotiations with the Republic. His justification is thin. His army is large.

timeline.title: Serath's forces meet Republic envoys at the Spine Bridge
A third unnamed army spotted in the southern marshes — no banners
timeline.desc: Scouts describe them as moving in complete silence. No campfires at night. No supply wagons.

timeline.title: Mira arrives at Kael's camp
Mira reveals she can now read the crown's emissions as text
timeline.desc: Not hear them, like Kael. Read them. As text. In a language she has never studied but now understands completely.

timeline.title: Chapter 2: The Weight of Permanence
timeline.desc: Kael tries to understand what the crown has already locked in place. Sorvenne helps. This is their most important conversation.

timeline.title: Sorvenne lists every irreversible consequence she knows of from the crown's history
timeline.desc: It takes four hours. Kael writes nothing down. He doesn't need to. The crown is already logging it.

timeline.title: Kael realises his exile 11 years ago was engineered — by Serath, to keep him away from the crown
Kael confronts Serath via messenger hawk — she replies: 'Yes.'
timeline.desc: He sends one question: 'Did you know what I was?' She sends back one word: 'Yes.'

timeline.title: Sorvenne reveals the only way to end the crown — Kael must die wearing it
Chapter 3: The Ashfield Convergence
timeline.desc: All threads arrive at the same place. The battle is not the climax — the choice before the battle is.

timeline.title: Vayne's army reaches the Ashfields first
The silent southern army is identified — they are the shadowless people of Delthara
timeline.desc: Delthara faded, but its people didn't. They followed Kael. No one asked them to.

timeline.title: Serath arrives alone — no army, no guard
Serath and Kael speak in person for the last time
timeline.desc: She apologises. Not for the exile — she'd do it again. For not telling him why. 'I was afraid you'd choose the crown on purpose.' He tells her she was right to be afraid.

timeline.title: Mira reads the crown's final inscription — it is a question, not a command
timeline.desc: The inscription asks: 'Do you choose this end, or do you accept it?' The difference, Mira explains, is everything.

timeline.title: Kael answers the crown out loud — in front of everyone
The Ashfields burn — consequences only, not people
timeline.desc: Every unjust exile. Every burned archive. Every broken treaty. Every choice made in the shadow of the crown burns away in about ninety seconds. The ground is left perfectly black and perfectly flat.

timeline.title: Kael is gone — only the crown remains, cold and dark for the first time
Epilogue: What Is Left
timeline.desc: No resolution. Just aftermath. What three people do on the first morning after.

timeline.title: Mira buries the crown without ceremony
Serath walks back toward the capital — alone, on foot
timeline.desc: She doesn't look back at the black field. She is already thinking about what governance looks like when there is no threat to organise around.

timeline.title: Sorvenne, finally without shadow, looks up at the sun
Book 5: PLACEHOLDER — Untitled Prequel
timeline.desc: NOTE FOR SELF: This book takes place 200 years before Book 1. Follows Sorvenne before the first burning of Delthara. Do not start writing this until Books 1-4 are locked. The timeline here will contradict Book 3 in at least two places — that is intentional and will need to be resolved in revision.

timeline.title: ROUGH NOTE: Sorvenne is born in Delthara — into the Remembrant collective
timeline.desc: She is the first child born in 40 years who does not immediately join the collective. The Remembrant does not know what to do with an individual.

timeline.title: ROUGH NOTE: She discovers fire reacts differently to her — she does not feel it as pain
ROUGH NOTE: The original crown is created — need to decide if Sorvenne witnesses or causes this
timeline.desc: Two versions of this scene exist in my notes. In one, she is a bystander. In the other, she is the one who brings the dead god's grief to the forge. Version 2 is better but makes her more complicit in everything that follows.

timeline.title: ROUGH NOTE: First burning of Delthara — Sorvenne's POV

parking.title: Interlude: The Cartographer's Account
parking.desc: A one-chapter interlude told through the field notes of a royal cartographer who witnesses the second quake from the eastern border. Pure worldbuilding — no named characters. Might cut entirely.

parking.title: Vayne's backstory scene — his sister died in the first quake
parking.desc: This was written to add sympathy for Vayne. Currently it makes him TOO sympathetic and undercuts the reader's ability to enjoy his eventual downfall. Parking until I figure out how much sympathy he deserves.

parking.title: Scene: Mira and Serath meet without Kael present
Spin-off Concept: The Republic Side
parking.desc: Told entirely from within the Silt Republic. None of the main characters appear. The reader sees the Ember Crown crisis as distant rumour and political opportunity. Very different in tone — more cynical, more bureaucratic, occasionally darkly funny. Not sure if this is a novella or a full book.

parking.title: Alternate ending: Kael survives but cannot speak — the crown takes his voice instead of his life
parking.desc: Thematically interesting. The man who answered the crown's question out loud in front of everyone loses the ability to speak. But it might be too clever. And Mira would have to become the de-facto narrator of the aftermath, which changes the whole epilogue structure.

parking.title: Chapter X: The Night Before the Ashfields
Sorvenne tells Kael about Aldren — the one ordinary person she loved before the burning
parking.desc: His name was Aldren. He was not special. That was the point. He was completely ordinary and she loved him for it and she burned the city and he was inside it. She has never said his name out loud since then. She says it here.

recycle.title: Chapter: The Dream Sequence
recycle.desc: Kael has a prophetic dream in which he sees all four books play out simultaneously. Cut because it spoiled too much, felt like a shortcut, and dreams-as-exposition is a cliche I don't want anywhere near this story.

recycle.title: Third character: a comic-relief merchant who travels with Kael for two chapters
recycle.desc: He was fun to write. He served no purpose. He was cut after draft one. His name was Botto.

recycle.title: Scene where the crown physically attacks Vayne
Book concept: The Second Crown
recycle.desc: There is a second crown. This was the original premise of the entire series before it was scrapped in outline phase. A second crown exists and the whole story is about two heirs, one per crown, working against each other. Abandoned because it made everything too symmetrical and removed the moral ambiguity from Sorvenne's role. Kept here as a reminder of why the current structure is better.

recycle.title: Mira is secretly working for Vayne — triple-agent subplot
recycle.desc: Cut in outline phase. Made her untrustworthy in a way that undercut every scene she shared with Kael. The story needs at least one person Kael can trust without question.
